title: Chapter 8 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of files and exceptions!

questions.question: What mode opens a file for reading?
questions.explanation: "r" is read mode — the default mode for open().

questions.question: The _____ statement automatically closes files.
questions.explanation: 'with' is a context manager that handles cleanup automatically.

questions.question: Write mode ("w") erases existing file content.
questions.explanation: "w" mode overwrites — existing content is lost!

questions.question: Which method reads the entire file at once?
questions.explanation: read() reads the entire file content as one string.

questions.question: The finally block only runs if an exception occurs.
questions.explanation: finally ALWAYS runs — whether try succeeds or fails!

questions.question: Which mode appends to a file without erasing?
questions.explanation: "a" is append mode — it adds to the end without erasing.

questions.question: What does try-except do?
questions.explanation: try-except catches exceptions so you can handle them gracefully.

questions.question: The _____ block runs only if no exception occurs.
questions.explanation: The else block in try-except runs when try succeeds.

questions.question: Does write() add newlines automatically?
questions.explanation: write() doesn't add newlines — you must include them yourself!

questions.question: 'with open()' is preferred over manual open/close.
questions.explanation: 'with' ensures cleanup even if errors occur — it's the best practice!

questions.question: What should you do when an error occurs?
questions.explanation: Use try-except to catch errors and handle them appropriately!
